warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble.
rules.evidenceQuote: The 'Codex General Standard for Food Additives' (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods...

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants to protect human health, which are legally permitted maximum concentrations. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.limit: Follow Code of Practice for prevention and reduction
rules.sourceTitle: Contaminants | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: A specific Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices CCCF 2017

rules.sourceTitle: Database - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: The database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and also process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 Gulf Technical Regulation - HALAL FOOD - Part 1 : General Requirements
rules.notes: General requirements for Halal food, applicable to seasoning and snack products, must be verified against the full text of GSO 2055-1:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 Gulf Technical Regulation - Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against the full text of GSO 9:2022.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 Gulf Technical Regulation - Expiration dates for food products -Part 1 : Mandatory expiration dates
rules.notes: Mandatory expiration date requirements for food products, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified against the full text of GSO 150-1:2013.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.parameter: Microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 1016:2015 Gulf Technical Regulation - Microbiological Criteria for Foodstuffs
rules.notes: Specific microbiological limits for foodstuffs and food ingredients, including those relevant to seasoning and snack products, must be verified against the full text of GSO 1016:2015.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.parameter: Permitted Food Additives and their Maximum Permitted Levels (MPL)
rules.limit: Verification required against FSS (Food Products Standards and Food Additives) Regulations, 2011 and subsequent amendments.
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Food Additives Regulations
rules.notes: The specific list of permitted food additives and their maximum permitted levels (MPL) for each food category must be verified by consulting the detailed schedules and provisions of the FSS (Food Products Standards and Food Additives) Regulations, 2011, and any subsequent amendments.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations_20_12_2022.pdf

rules.limit: Verification required for specific allergens and declaration format as per FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020.
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: Food products containing specified allergens (e.g., milk, soy, wheat, nuts) must declare them prominently. The exact list of allergens and the prescribed format for declaration must be verified by consulting the FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020, and its amendments.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display_30_06_2022.pdf

rules.parameter: Front-of-Pack Labelling (FoPL) for High Fat, Sugar, Sodium (HFSS)
rules.limit: Verification required for thresholds and warning format as per FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020.
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: Processed food products, including snacks, that exceed specified thresholds for fat, sugar, or sodium are required to carry mandatory front-of-pack warning labels. The exact thresholds and the prescribed warning format must be verified by consulting the FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020, and its amendments.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display_30_06_2022.pdf

rules.limit: Verification required for all mandatory labelling elements as per FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020.
rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: All pre-packaged food products must display essential information such as the name of the food, ingredients list, nutritional information, net quantity, FSSAI License number, manufacturer details, date markings, and instructions for use. Specific requirements for display and content must be verified by consulting the FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020, and its amendments.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display_30_06_2022.pdf

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for food additives in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant BPOM regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: All processed food products, including seasoning and snack products, must comply with the regulations issued by Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan (BPOM) of Indonesia. Specific regulations must be consulted for detailed requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: Specific requirements for labelling warnings and other general labelling information for processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant BPOM regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for microbiological contaminants in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant BPOM regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: Jaringan Dokumentasi dan Informasi Hukum Badan Pengawas Obat dan Makanan RI
rules.notes: Specific limits for salt/sodium content in processed foods, including seasoning and snack products, must be verified by consulting the relevant BPOM regulations under the 'Pangan Olahan' category.
rules.evidenceQuote: 281 Peraturan Pangan Olahan

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of specific food additive regulations is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Additives

rules.parameter: Specifications and Standards for Food and Food Additives
rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of specific specifications and standards for food and food additives under the Food Sanitation Act is required. This document also covers microbiology and contaminant limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Compliance with the Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of BSE-related food safety measures is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy(BSE)

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of regulations on food contaminated by radioactivity is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Handling of food contaminated by radioactivity

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Compliance with the Food Sanitation Law is required, which serves as a foundational legal framework for food safety, including additives, contaminants, and labelling.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Sanitation Law

rules.parameter: Health Claims, Special Dietary Uses, Nutrition Labeling
rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Verification of regulations concerning health claims, food for special dietary uses, and nutrition labeling is required.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food with Health Claims, Food for Special Dietary Uses, and Nutrition Labeling

rules.sourceTitle: Food Regulations 1985
rules.notes: The provided 'TEXT' is a PDF stream and cannot be directly processed to extract regulatory rules. Please provide the actual text content of the regulations for extraction.
rules.evidenceQuote: The provided text is a PDF stream and cannot be directly processed.

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 Technical Regulation --> Labels of prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.notes: Specific labelling requirements, including warnings and allergen declarations, must be verified by consulting the full technical regulation GSO 9:2022.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used, the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food... use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Limits depend on the additive and food product type, requiring reference to the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products. Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Import or sale of food products containing non-permitted additives is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.notes: Verification can be done using the SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses must comply with the regulatory limits on incidental constituents i.e. contaminants in food.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Import or sale of food products containing contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits is prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.parameter: Specific contaminant limits (microbiological, heavy metals, mycotoxins, marine biotoxins, veterinary drug residues, pesticide residues, other incidental constituents)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Verification requires referring to relevant SFA documents for specific limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues apply to various food commodities and can be downloaded from the SFA website.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Food Additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Your food or food product must also comply with the labelling requirements.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Microbiological standards apply to both ready-to-eat and non-ready-to-eat food, requiring reference to specific SFA documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards Ready-to-eat food Non-ready-to-eat food (till 3 Mar 2024) Non-ready-to-eat food (from 4 Mar 2024)

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: Specific use restrictions and labeling requirements must be verified against the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products, Spiral shells and Spiral shell products, Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined/purified sunflower seed oil), Kiwis and kiwi products

rules.notes: Labeling for these allergens is recommended if present as an ingredient or food additive.

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These allergens require mandatory labeling if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Sulphite at or above this concentration requires mandatory labeling.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits must be verified against the full standard.

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims must be verified against the full regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A precautionary statement is suggested if there is a risk of allergen cross-contamination during production.
rules.evidenceQuote: the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA; Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Color additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 73, 74). The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (...and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Delisted color additives are in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
rules.evidenceQuote: Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings | FDA; Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Food additives must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173). The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173...)

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: GRAS substances must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).
rules.evidenceQuote: Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: A threshold is a value below which it is unlikely that a food allergic individual would experience an adverse effect.
rules.evidenceQuote: At this time, the FDA has not established a threshold level for any allergens.

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: These are the nine major food allergens recognized by U.S. law (FALCPA and FASTER Act). Sesame became the 9th major allergen effective January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. ...declaring sesame as the 9th major food allergen recognized by the United States. The change was effective on January 1, 2023.

rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: This is an Action Level for total aflatoxins in foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.parameter: Chemical contaminants (Toxic Elements, Mycotoxins, Persistent Pesticides, Radionuclides, Other Chemical Contaminants)
rules.sourceTitle: Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool
rules.notes: Contaminant levels (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, etc.) are provided in the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and related guidance documents and regulations. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) parts 109 and 509.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the 'gluten-free' claim were issued on August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling, which helps consumers, especially those living with celiac disease, be confident that items labeled “gluten-free” meet a defined standard for gluten content.

rules.limit: Not required by law, not a substitute for GMPs
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Such statements can be used to address unavoidable cross-contact only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes and taken every precaution.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact...

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The FDA's "Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food" rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls.
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required by the Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act of 2004 (FALCPA).
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.parameter: Declaration of specific types/species for certain allergens
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The specific type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.parameter: Format for Major Food Allergen Declaration
rules.limit: Must appear in parentheses after ingredient name OR in a "contains" statement
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label.
rules.evidenceQuote: The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. ... OR ... Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.parameter: Products with undeclared allergens or lacking required allergen information
rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Such products are subject to enforcement actions like recalls, import refusal, and seizure.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA considers such products misbranded or adulterated, depending on the circumstances, and subject to enforcement actions such as recalls, import refusal, and seizure.

rules.sourceTitle: Food Allergies | FDA
rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present at or above this concentration due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
